title: 金鯱賞の後に・足元から伸びる影

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Mwahahaha! That's all there is to it! Well, what do
you think, Trainer?

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
How impressed are you?! Go on, you can praise
me as much as you want!

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Whatever could you mean, Suzuka? As you can
see, I'm absolutely sublime.

text_block_list.name: Silence Suzuka
text_block_list.text:
You ran fantastically, but... your eyes look
different.

text_block_list.name: Silence Suzuka
text_block_list.text:
It's almost like you're a different person. Like
you've been swept away by something.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
Engulfed? The only thing I can possibly think of
being engulfed by... is good fortune. Is that a bad
thing?

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
It's true I'm not really acting like myself. I've been
letting luck lead me along and control my actions.

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
I mean, I'm not really doing anything. I just go
wherever the tides of fortune take me...

text_block_list.name: Matikanefukukitaru
text_block_list.text:
It's like I'm possessed by a fox spirit... I've forked
over my entire being just to win...

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
These thoughts stop her dead in her tracks. The
luck that she was always faithful to appears to be
turning into something dark and sinister.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I tell her that our next goal is the Takarazuka
Kinen. She doesn't respond.
